NKJV.1: Then He called His twelve disciples together and gave them power and authority over all demons, and to cure diseases.
NKJV.2: He sent them to preach the kingdom of God and to heal the sick.
NKJV.3: And He said to them, “Take nothing for the journey, neither staffs nor bag nor bread nor money; and do not have two tunics apiece.
NKJV.4: “Whatever house you enter, stay there, and from there depart.
NKJV.5: And whoever will not receive you, when you go out of that city,  shake off the very dust from your feet as a testimony against them.”
NKJV.6: So they departed and went through the towns, preaching the gospel and healing everywhere.
NKJV.7: Now Herod the tetrarch heard of all that was done by Him; and he was perplexed, because it was said by some that John had risen from the dead,
NKJV.8: and by some that Elijah had appeared, and by others that one of the old prophets had risen again.
NKJV.9: Herod said, “John I have beheaded, but who is this of whom I hear such things?” So he sought to see Him.
NKJV.10: And the apostles, when they had returned, told Him all that they had done. Then He took them and went aside privately into a deserted place belonging to the city called Bethsaida.
NKJV.11: But when the multitudes knew it, they followed Him; and He received them and spoke to them about the kingdom of God, and healed those who had need of healing.
NKJV.12: When the day began to wear away, the twelve came and said to Him, “Send the multitude away, that they may go into the surrounding towns and country, and lodge and get provisions; for we are in a deserted place here.”
NKJV.13: But He said to them, “You give them something to eat.” And they said, “We have no more than five loaves and two fish, unless we go and buy food for all these people.”
NKJV.14: For there were about five thousand men. Then He said to His disciples, “Make them sit down in groups of fifty.”
NKJV.15: And they did so, and made them all sit down.
NKJV.16: Then He took the five loaves and the two fish, and looking up to heaven, He blessed and broke them, and gave them to the disciples to set before the multitude.
NKJV.17: So they all ate and were filled, and twelve baskets of the leftover fragments were taken up by them.
NKJV.18: And it happened, as He was alone praying, that His disciples joined Him, and He asked them, saying, “Who do the crowds say that I am?”
NKJV.19: So they answered and said, “John the Baptist, but some say Elijah; and others say that one of the old prophets has risen again.”
NKJV.20: He said to them, “But who do you say that I am?”  Peter answered and said, “The Christ of God.”
NKJV.21: And He strictly warned and commanded them to tell this to no one,
NKJV.22: saying, “The Son of Man must suffer many things, and be rejected by the elders and chief priests and scribes, and be killed, and be raised the third day.”
NKJV.23: Then He said to them all, “If anyone desires to come after Me, let him deny himself, and take up his cross daily, and follow Me.
NKJV.24: For whoever desires to save his life will lose it, but whoever loses his life for My sake will save it.
NKJV.25: For what profit is it to a man if he gains the whole world, and is himself destroyed or lost?
NKJV.26: For whoever is ashamed of Me and My words, of him the Son of Man will be ashamed when He comes in His own glory, and in His Father’s, and of the holy angels.
NKJV.27: But I tell you truly, there are some standing here who shall not taste death till they see the kingdom of God.”
NKJV.28: Now it came to pass, about eight days after these sayings, that He took Peter, John, and James and went up on the mountain to pray.
NKJV.29: As He prayed, the appearance of His face was altered, and His robe became white and glistening.
NKJV.30: And behold, two men talked with Him, who were Moses and Elijah,
NKJV.31: who appeared in glory and spoke of His decease which He was about to accomplish at Jerusalem.
NKJV.32: But Peter and those with him were heavy with sleep; and when they were fully awake, they saw His glory and the two men who stood with Him.
NKJV.33: Then it happened, as they were parting from Him, that Peter said to Jesus, “Master, it is good for us to be here; and let us make three tabernacles: one for You, one for Moses, and one for Elijah”—not knowing what he said.
NKJV.34: While he was saying this, a cloud came and overshadowed them; and they were fearful as they entered the cloud.
NKJV.35: And a voice came out of the cloud, saying, “This is My beloved Son. Hear Him!”
NKJV.36: When the voice had ceased, Jesus was found alone. But they kept quiet, and told no one in those days any of the things they had seen.
NKJV.37: Now it happened on the next day, when they had come down from the mountain, that a great multitude met Him.
NKJV.38: Suddenly a man from the multitude cried out, saying, “Teacher, I implore You, look on my son, for he is my only child.
NKJV.39: And behold, a spirit seizes him, and he suddenly cries out; it convulses him so that he foams at the mouth; and it departs from him with great difficulty, bruising him.
NKJV.40: So I implored Your disciples to cast it out, but they could not.”
NKJV.41: Then Jesus answered and said, “O faithless and perverse generation, how long shall I be with you and bear with you? Bring your son here.”
NKJV.42: And as he was still coming, the demon threw him down and convulsed him. Then Jesus rebuked the unclean spirit, healed the child, and gave him back to his father.
NKJV.43: And they were all amazed at the majesty of God. But while everyone marveled at all the things which Jesus did, He said to His disciples,
NKJV.44: “Let these words sink down into your ears, for the Son of Man is about to be betrayed into the hands of men.”
NKJV.45: But they did not understand this saying, and it was hidden from them so that they did not perceive it; and they were afraid to ask Him about this saying.
NKJV.46: Then a dispute arose among them as to which of them would be greatest.
NKJV.47: And Jesus, perceiving the thought of their heart, took a little child and set him by Him,
NKJV.48: and said to them, “Whoever receives this little child in My name receives Me; and  whoever receives Me  receives Him who sent Me.  For he who is least among you all will be great.”
NKJV.49: Now John answered and said, “Master, we saw someone casting out demons in Your name, and we forbade him because he does not follow with us.”
NKJV.50: But Jesus said to him, “Do not forbid him, for he who is not against us is on our side.”
NKJV.51: Now it came to pass, when the time had come for Him to be received up, that He steadfastly set His face to go to Jerusalem,
NKJV.52: and sent messengers before His face. And as they went, they entered a village of the Samaritans, to prepare for Him.
NKJV.53: But they did not receive Him, because His face was set for the journey to Jerusalem.
NKJV.54: And when His disciples James and John saw this, they said, “Lord, do You want us to command fire to come down from heaven and consume them, just as Elijah did?”
NKJV.55: But He turned and rebuked them, and said, “You do not know what manner of  spirit you are of.
NKJV.56: For the Son of Man did not come to destroy men’s lives but to save them.” And they went to another village.
NKJV.57: Now it happened as they journeyed on the road, that someone said to Him, “Lord, I will follow You wherever You go.”
NKJV.58: And Jesus said to him, “Foxes have holes and birds of the air have nests, but the Son of Man has nowhere to lay His head.”
NKJV.59: Then He said to another, “Follow Me.” But he said, “Lord, let me first go and bury my father.”
NKJV.60: Jesus said to him, “Let the dead bury their own dead, but you go and preach the kingdom of God.”
NKJV.61: And another also said, “Lord, I will follow You, but let me first go and bid them farewell who are at my house.”
NKJV.62: But Jesus said to him, “No one, having put his hand to the plow, and looking back, is  fit for the kingdom of God.”

ESV.1: And he called the twelve together and gave them power and authority over all demons and to cure diseases,
ESV.2: and he sent them out to proclaim the kingdom of God and to heal.
ESV.3: And he said to them, “Take nothing for your journey, no staff, nor bag, nor bread, nor money; and do not have two tunics.
ESV.4: And whatever house you enter, stay there, and from there depart.
ESV.5: And wherever they do not receive you, when you leave that town shake off the dust from your feet as a testimony against them.”
ESV.6: And they departed and went through the villages, preaching the gospel and healing everywhere.
ESV.7: Now Herod the tetrarch heard about all that was happening, and he was perplexed, because it was said by some that John had been raised from the dead,
ESV.8: by some that Elijah had appeared, and by others that one of the prophets of old had risen.
ESV.9: Herod said, “John I beheaded, but who is this about whom I hear such things?” And he sought to see him.
ESV.10: On their return the apostles told him all that they had done. And he took them and withdrew apart to a town called Bethsaida.
ESV.11: When the crowds learned it, they followed him, and he welcomed them and spoke to them of the kingdom of God and cured those who had need of healing.
ESV.12: Now the day began to wear away, and the twelve came and said to him, “Send the crowd away to go into the surrounding villages and countryside to find lodging and get provisions, for we are here in a desolate place.”
ESV.13: But he said to them,  “You give them something to eat.” They said, “We have no more than five loaves and two fish — unless we are to go and buy food for all these people.”
ESV.14: For there were about five thousand men. And he said to his disciples, “Have them sit down in groups of about fifty each.”
ESV.15: And they did so, and had them all sit down.
ESV.16: And taking the five loaves and the two fish, he looked up to heaven and said a blessing over them. Then he broke the loaves and gave them to the disciples to set before the crowd.
ESV.17: And they all ate and were satisfied. And what was left over was picked up, twelve baskets of broken pieces.
ESV.18: Now it happened that as he was praying alone, the disciples were with him. And he asked them, “Who do the crowds say that I am?”
ESV.19: And they answered, “John the Baptist. But others say, Elijah, and others, that one of the prophets of old has risen.”
ESV.20: Then he said to them, “But who do you say that I am?” And Peter answered, “The Christ of God.”
ESV.21: And he strictly charged and commanded them to tell this to no one,
ESV.22: saying,  “The Son of Man must suffer many things and be rejected by the elders and chief priests and scribes, and be killed, and on the third day be raised.”
ESV.23: And he said to all, “If anyone would come after me, let him deny himself and take up his cross daily and follow me.
ESV.24: For whoever would save his life will lose it, but whoever loses his life for my sake will save it.
ESV.25: For what does it profit a man if he gains the whole world and loses or forfeits himself?
ESV.26: For whoever is ashamed of me and of my words, of him will the Son of Man be ashamed when he comes in his glory and the glory of the Father and of the holy angels.
ESV.27: But I tell you truly, there are some standing here who will not taste death until they see the kingdom of God.”
ESV.28: Now about eight days after these sayings he took with him Peter and John and James and went up on the mountain to pray.
ESV.29: And as he was praying, the appearance of his face was altered, and his clothing became dazzling white.
ESV.30: And behold, two men were talking with him, Moses and Elijah,
ESV.31: who appeared in glory and spoke of his departure,  which he was about to accomplish at Jerusalem.
ESV.32: Now Peter and those who were with him were heavy with sleep, but when they became fully awake they saw his glory and the two men who stood with him.
ESV.33: And as the men were parting from him, Peter said to Jesus, “Master, it is good that we are here. Let us make three tents, one for you and one for Moses and one for Elijah” — not knowing what he said.
ESV.34: As he was saying these things, a cloud came and overshadowed them, and they were afraid as they entered the cloud.
ESV.35: And a voice came out of the cloud, saying, “This is my Son, my Chosen One;  listen to him!”
ESV.36: And when the voice had spoken, Jesus was found alone. And they kept silent and told no one in those days anything of what they had seen.
ESV.37: On the next day, when they had come down from the mountain, a great crowd met him.
ESV.38: And behold, a man from the crowd cried out, “Teacher, I beg you to look at my son, for he is my only child.
ESV.39: And behold, a spirit seizes him, and he suddenly cries out. It convulses him so that he foams at the mouth, and shatters him, and will hardly leave him.
ESV.40: And I begged your disciples to cast it out, but they could not.”
ESV.41: Jesus answered, “O faithless and twisted generation, how long am I to be with you and bear with you? Bring your son here.”
ESV.42: While he was coming, the demon threw him to the ground and convulsed him. But Jesus rebuked the unclean spirit and healed the boy, and gave him back to his father.
ESV.43: And all were astonished at the majesty of God. Jesus Again Foretells His Death But while they were all marveling at everything he was doing, Jesus said to his disciples,
ESV.44: “Let these words sink into your ears: The Son of Man is about to be delivered into the hands of men.”
ESV.45: But they did not understand this saying, and it was concealed from them, so that they might not perceive it. And they were afraid to ask him about this saying.
ESV.46: An argument arose among them as to which of them was the greatest.
ESV.47: But Jesus, knowing the reasoning of their hearts, took a child and put him by his side
ESV.48: and said to them,  “Whoever receives this child in my name receives me, and whoever receives me receives him who sent me. For he who is least among you all is the one who is great.”
ESV.49: John answered, “Master, we saw someone casting out demons in your name, and we tried to stop him, because he does not follow with us.”
ESV.50: But Jesus said to him, “Do not stop him, for the one who is not against you is for you.”
ESV.51: When the days drew near for him to be taken up, he set his face to go to Jerusalem.
ESV.52: And he sent messengers ahead of him, who went and entered a village of the Samaritans, to make preparations for him.
ESV.53: But the people did not receive him, because his face was set toward Jerusalem.
ESV.54: And when his disciples James and John saw it, they said, “Lord, do you want us to tell fire to come down from heaven and consume them?”
ESV.55: But he turned and rebuked them.
ESV.56: And they went on to another village.
ESV.57: As they were going along the road, someone said to him, “I will follow you wherever you go.”
ESV.58: And Jesus said to him, “Foxes have holes, and birds of the air have nests, but the Son of Man has nowhere to lay his head.”
ESV.59: To another he said, “Follow me.” But he said, “Lord, let me first go and bury my father.”
ESV.60: And Jesus  said to him, “Leave the dead to bury their own dead. But as for you, go and proclaim the kingdom of God.”
ESV.61: Yet another said, “I will follow you, Lord, but let me first say farewell to those at my home.”
ESV.62: Jesus said to him,  “No one who puts his hand to the plow and looks back is fit for the kingdom of God.”

NASB.44: 'Let these words sink into your ears; for the Son of Man is going to be delivered into the hands of men.'
NASB.45: But they did not understand this statement, and it was concealed from them so that they would not perceive it; and they were afraid to ask Him about this statement.
NASB.46: An argument started among them as to which of them might be the greatest.
NASB.47: But Jesus, knowing what they were thinking in their heart, took a child and stood him by His side,
NASB.48: and said to them, 'Whoever receives this child in My name receives Me, and whoever receives Me receives Him who sent Me; for the one who is least among all of you, this is the one who is great.'
NASB.49: John answered and said, 'Master, we saw someone casting out demons in Your name; and we tried to prevent him because he does not follow along with us.'
NASB.50: But Jesus said to him, 'Do not hinder [him]; for he who is not against you is for you.'
NASB.51: When the days were approaching for His ascension, He was determined to go to Jerusalem;
NASB.52: and He sent messengers on ahead of Him, and they went and entered a village of the Samaritans to make arrangements for Him.
NASB.53: But they did not receive Him, because He was traveling toward Jerusalem.
NASB.54: When His disciples James and John saw [this], they said, 'Lord, do You want us to command fire to come down from heaven and consume them?'
NASB.55: But He turned and rebuked them, [and said, 'You do not know what kind of spirit you are of;
NASB.56: for the Son of Man did not come to destroy men's lives, but to save them.]' And they went on to another village.
NASB.57: As they were going along the road, someone said to Him, 'I will follow You wherever You go.'
NASB.58: And Jesus said to him, 'The foxes have holes and the birds of the air [have] nests, but the Son of Man has nowhere to lay His head.'
NASB.59: And He said to another, 'Follow Me.' But he said, 'Lord, permit me first to go and bury my father.'
NASB.60: But He said to him, 'Allow the dead to bury their own dead; but as for you, go and proclaim everywhere the kingdom of God.'
NASB.61: Another also said, 'I will follow You, Lord; but first permit me to say good-bye to those at home.'
NASB.62: But Jesus said to him, 'No one, after putting his hand to the plow and looking back, is fit for the kingdom of God.'

NLT.1: One day Jesus called together his twelve disciples and gave them power and authority to cast out all demons and to heal all diseases.
NLT.2: Then he sent them out to tell everyone about the Kingdom of God and to heal the sick.
NLT.3: “Take nothing for your journey,” he instructed them. “Don’t take a walking stick, a traveler’s bag, food, money, or even a change of clothes.
NLT.4: Wherever you go, stay in the same house until you leave town.
NLT.5: And if a town refuses to welcome you, shake its dust from your feet as you leave to show that you have abandoned those people to their fate.”
NLT.6: So they began their circuit of the villages, preaching the Good News and healing the sick.
NLT.7: When Herod Antipas, the ruler of Galilee, heard about everything Jesus was doing, he was puzzled. Some were saying that John the Baptist had been raised from the dead.
NLT.8: Others thought Jesus was Elijah or one of the other prophets risen from the dead.
NLT.9: “I beheaded John,” Herod said, “so who is this man about whom I hear such stories?” And he kept trying to see him.
NLT.10: When the apostles returned, they told Jesus everything they had done. Then he slipped quietly away with them toward the town of Bethsaida.
NLT.11: But the crowds found out where he was going, and they followed him. He welcomed them and taught them about the Kingdom of God, and he healed those who were sick.
NLT.12: Late in the afternoon the twelve disciples came to him and said, “Send the crowds away to the nearby villages and farms, so they can find food and lodging for the night. There is nothing to eat here in this remote place.”
NLT.13: But Jesus said, “You feed them.” “But we have only five loaves of bread and two fish,” they answered. “Or are you expecting us to go and buy enough food for this whole crowd?”
NLT.14: For there were about 5,000 men there. Jesus replied, “Tell them to sit down in groups of about fifty each.”
NLT.15: So the people all sat down.
NLT.16: Jesus took the five loaves and two fish, looked up toward heaven, and blessed them. Then, breaking the loaves into pieces, he kept giving the bread and fish to the disciples so they could distribute it to the people.
NLT.17: They all ate as much as they wanted, and afterward, the disciples picked up twelve baskets of leftovers!
NLT.18: One day Jesus left the crowds to pray alone. Only his disciples were with him, and he asked them, “Who do people say I am?”
NLT.19: “Well,” they replied, “some say John the Baptist, some say Elijah, and others say you are one of the other ancient prophets risen from the dead.”
NLT.20: Then he asked them, “But who do you say I am?” Peter replied, “You are the Messiah sent from God!”
NLT.21: Jesus warned his disciples not to tell anyone who he was.
NLT.22: “The Son of Man must suffer many terrible things,” he said. “He will be rejected by the elders, the leading priests, and the teachers of religious law. He will be killed, but on the third day he will be raised from the dead.”
NLT.23: Then he said to the crowd, “If any of you wants to be my follower, you must give up your own way, take up your cross daily, and follow me.
NLT.24: If you try to hang on to your life, you will lose it. But if you give up your life for my sake, you will save it.
NLT.25: And what do you benefit if you gain the whole world but are yourself lost or destroyed?
NLT.26: If anyone is ashamed of me and my message, the Son of Man will be ashamed of that person when he returns in his glory and in the glory of the Father and the holy angels.
NLT.27: I tell you the truth, some standing here right now will not die before they see the Kingdom of God.”
NLT.28: About eight days later Jesus took Peter, John, and James up on a mountain to pray.
NLT.29: And as he was praying, the appearance of his face was transformed, and his clothes became dazzling white.
NLT.30: Suddenly, two men, Moses and Elijah, appeared and began talking with Jesus.
NLT.31: They were glorious to see. And they were speaking about his exodus from this world, which was about to be fulfilled in Jerusalem.
NLT.32: Peter and the others had fallen asleep. When they woke up, they saw Jesus’ glory and the two men standing with him.
NLT.33: As Moses and Elijah were starting to leave, Peter, not even knowing what he was saying, blurted out, “Master, it’s wonderful for us to be here! Let’s make three shelters as memorials — one for you, one for Moses, and one for Elijah.”
NLT.34: But even as he was saying this, a cloud overshadowed them, and terror gripped them as the cloud covered them.
NLT.35: Then a voice from the cloud said, “This is my Son, my Chosen One. Listen to him.”
NLT.36: When the voice finished, Jesus was there alone. They didn’t tell anyone at that time what they had seen.
NLT.37: The next day, after they had come down the mountain, a large crowd met Jesus.
NLT.38: A man in the crowd called out to him, “Teacher, I beg you to look at my son, my only child.
NLT.39: An evil spirit keeps seizing him, making him scream. It throws him into convulsions so that he foams at the mouth. It batters him and hardly ever leaves him alone.
NLT.40: I begged your disciples to cast out the spirit, but they couldn’t do it.”
NLT.41: Jesus said, “You faithless and corrupt people! How long must I be with you and put up with you?” Then he said to the man, “Bring your son here.”
NLT.42: As the boy came forward, the demon knocked him to the ground and threw him into a violent convulsion. But Jesus rebuked the evil spirit and healed the boy. Then he gave him back to his father.
NLT.43: Awe gripped the people as they saw this majestic display of God’s power. Jesus Again Predicts His Death While everyone was marveling at everything he was doing, Jesus said to his disciples,
NLT.44: “Listen to me and remember what I say. The Son of Man is going to be betrayed into the hands of his enemies.”
NLT.45: But they didn’t know what he meant. Its significance was hidden from them, so they couldn’t understand it, and they were afraid to ask him about it.
NLT.46: Then his disciples began arguing about which of them was the greatest.
NLT.47: But Jesus knew their thoughts, so he brought a little child to his side.
NLT.48: Then he said to them, “Anyone who welcomes a little child like this on my behalf welcomes me, and anyone who welcomes me also welcomes my Father who sent me. Whoever is the least among you is the greatest.”
NLT.49: John said to Jesus, “Master, we saw someone using your name to cast out demons, but we told him to stop because he isn’t in our group.”
NLT.50: But Jesus said, “Don’t stop him! Anyone who is not against you is for you.”
NLT.51: As the time drew near for him to ascend to heaven, Jesus resolutely set out for Jerusalem.
NLT.52: He sent messengers ahead to a Samaritan village to prepare for his arrival.
NLT.53: But the people of the village did not welcome Jesus because he was on his way to Jerusalem.
NLT.54: When James and John saw this, they said to Jesus, “Lord, should we call down fire from heaven to burn them up ?”
NLT.55: But Jesus turned and rebuked them.
NLT.56: So they went on to another village.
NLT.57: As they were walking along, someone said to Jesus, “I will follow you wherever you go.”
NLT.58: But Jesus replied, “Foxes have dens to live in, and birds have nests, but the Son of Man has no place even to lay his head.”
NLT.59: He said to another person, “Come, follow me.” The man agreed, but he said, “Lord, first let me return home and bury my father.”
NLT.60: But Jesus told him, “Let the spiritually dead bury their own dead! Your duty is to go and preach about the Kingdom of God.”
NLT.61: Another said, “Yes, Lord, I will follow you, but first let me say good-bye to my family.”
NLT.62: But Jesus told him, “Anyone who puts a hand to the plow and then looks back is not fit for the Kingdom of God.”

WEB.1: He called the twelve  together, and gave them power and authority over all demons, and to cure diseases.
WEB.2: He sent them out to preach God’s Kingdom and to heal the sick.
WEB.3: He said to them, “Take nothing for your journey — neither staffs, nor wallet, nor bread, nor money; neither have two coats apiece.
WEB.4: Into whatever house you enter, stay there, and depart from there.
WEB.5: As many as don’t receive you, when you depart from that city, shake off even the dust from your feet for a testimony against them.”
WEB.6: They departed, and went throughout the villages, preaching the Good News, and healing everywhere.
WEB.7: Now Herod the tetrarch heard of all that was done by him; and he was very perplexed, because it was said by some that John had risen from the dead,
WEB.8: and by some that Elijah had appeared, and by others that one of the old prophets had risen again.
WEB.9: Herod said, “John I beheaded, but who is this, about whom I hear such things?” He sought to see him.
WEB.10: The apostles, when they had returned, told him what things they had done. He took them, and withdrew apart to a deserted place of a city called Bethsaida.
WEB.11: But the multitudes, perceiving it, followed him. He welcomed them, and spoke to them of God’s Kingdom, and he cured those who needed healing.
WEB.12: The day began to wear away; and the twelve came, and said to him, “Send the multitude away, that they may go into the surrounding villages and farms, and lodge, and get food, for we are here in a deserted place.”
WEB.13: But he said to them, “You give them something to eat.” They said, “We have no more than five loaves and two fish, unless we should go and buy food for all these people.”
WEB.14: For they were about five thousand men. He said to his disciples, “Make them sit down in groups of about fifty each.”
WEB.15: They did so, and made them all sit down.
WEB.16: He took the five loaves and the two fish, and looking up to the sky, he blessed them, and broke them, and gave them to the disciples to set before the multitude.
WEB.17: They ate, and were all filled. They gathered up twelve baskets of broken pieces that were left over.
WEB.18: As he was praying alone, the disciples were with him, and he asked them, “Who do the multitudes say that I am?”
WEB.19: They answered, “‘John the Baptizer,’ but others say, ‘Elijah,’ and others, that one of the old prophets is risen again.”
WEB.20: He said to them, “But who do you say that I am?” Peter answered, “The Christ of God.”
WEB.21: But he warned them, and commanded them to tell this to no one,
WEB.22: saying, “The Son of Man must suffer many things, and be rejected by the elders, chief priests, and scribes, and be killed, and the third day be raised up.”
WEB.23: He said to all, “If anyone desires to come after me, let him deny himself, take up his cross,  and follow me.
WEB.24: For whoever desires to save his life will lose it, but whoever will lose his life for my sake, the same will save it.
WEB.25: For what does it profit a man if he gains the whole world, and loses or forfeits his own self?
WEB.26: For whoever will be ashamed of me and of my words, of him will the Son of Man be ashamed, when he comes in his glory, and the glory of the Father, and of the holy angels.
WEB.27: But I tell you the truth: There are some of those who stand here, who will in no way taste of death, until they see God’s Kingdom.”
WEB.28: About eight days after these sayings, he took with him Peter, John, and James, and went up onto the mountain to pray.
WEB.29: As he was praying, the appearance of his face was altered, and his clothing became white and dazzling.
WEB.30: Behold, two men were talking with him, who were Moses and Elijah,
WEB.31: who appeared in glory, and spoke of his departure,  which he was about to accomplish at Jerusalem.
WEB.32: Now Peter and those who were with him were heavy with sleep, but when they were fully awake, they saw his glory, and the two men who stood with him.
WEB.33: As they were parting from him, Peter said to Jesus, “Master, it is good for us to be here. Let’s make three tents: one for you, and one for Moses, and one for Elijah,” not knowing what he said.
WEB.34: While he said these things, a cloud came and overshadowed them, and they were afraid as they entered into the cloud.
WEB.35: A voice came out of the cloud, saying, “This is my beloved Son. Listen to him!”
WEB.36: When the voice came, Jesus was found alone. They were silent, and told no one in those days any of the things which they had seen.
WEB.37: On the next day, when they had come down from the mountain, a great multitude met him.
WEB.38: Behold, a man from the crowd called out, saying, “Teacher, I beg you to look at my son, for he is my only child.
WEB.39: Behold, a spirit takes him, he suddenly cries out, and it convulses him so that he foams, and it hardly departs from him, bruising him severely.
WEB.40: I begged your disciples to cast it out, and they couldn’t.”
WEB.41: Jesus answered, “Faithless and perverse generation, how long shall I be with you and bear with you? Bring your son here.”
WEB.42: While he was still coming, the demon threw him down and convulsed him violently. But Jesus rebuked the unclean spirit, and healed the boy, and gave him back to his father.
WEB.43: They were all astonished at the majesty of God. But while all were marveling at all the things which Jesus did, he said to his disciples,
WEB.44: “Let these words sink into your ears, for the Son of Man will be delivered up into the hands of men.”
WEB.45: But they didn’t understand this saying. It was concealed from them, that they should not perceive it, and they were afraid to ask him about this saying.
WEB.46: There arose an argument among them about which of them was the greatest.
WEB.47: Jesus, perceiving the reasoning of their hearts, took a little child, and set him by his side,
WEB.48: and said to them, “Whoever receives this little child in my name receives me. Whoever receives me receives him who sent me. For whoever is least among you all, this one will be great.”
WEB.49: John answered, “Master, we saw someone casting out demons in your name, and we forbade him, because he doesn’t follow with us.”
WEB.50: Jesus said to him, “Don’t forbid him, for he who is not against us is for us.”
WEB.51: It came to pass, when the days were near that he should be taken up, he intently set his face to go to Jerusalem,
WEB.52: and sent messengers before his face. They went, and entered into a village of the Samaritans, so as to prepare for him.
WEB.53: They didn’t receive him, because he was traveling with his face set towards Jerusalem.
WEB.54: When his disciples, James and John, saw this, they said, “Lord, do you want us to command fire to come down from the sky, and destroy them, just as Elijah did?”
WEB.55: But he turned and rebuked them, “You don’t know of what kind of spirit you are.
WEB.56: For the Son of Man didn’t come to destroy men’s lives, but to save them.” They went to another village.
WEB.57: As they went on the way, a certain man said to him, “I want to follow you wherever you go, Lord.”
WEB.58: Jesus said to him, “The foxes have holes, and the birds of the sky have nests, but the Son of Man has no place to lay his head.”
WEB.59: He said to another, “Follow me!” But he said, “Lord, allow me first to go and bury my father.”
WEB.60: But Jesus said to him, “Leave the dead to bury their own dead, but you go and announce God’s Kingdom.”
WEB.61: Another also said, “I want to follow you, Lord, but first allow me to say good-bye to those who are at my house.”
WEB.62: But Jesus said to him, “No one, having put his hand to the plow, and looking back, is fit for God’s Kingdom.”

AMP.1: Now Jesus called together the twelve [disciples] and gave them [the right to exercise] power and authority over all the demons and to heal diseases.
AMP.2: Then He sent them out [on a brief journey] to preach the kingdom of God and to perform healing.
AMP.3: And He said to them, "Take nothing for your journey [that might encumber you]—neither a walking stick, nor bag, nor bread, nor money; and do not even have two tunics apiece.
AMP.4: Whatever house you enter, stay there until you leave that city [to go to another].
AMP.5: And as for all those who do not welcome you, when you leave that city, shake the dust off your feet [breaking all ties with them] as a testimony against them [that they rejected My message]."
AMP.6: So they began going from village to village, preaching the gospel and healing the sick everywhere.
AMP.7: Now Herod [Antipas] the tetrarch [who governed a portion of Palestine including Galilee and Perea] heard about all that was being done [by Jesus], and he was thoroughly perplexed, because it was said by some that John [the Baptist whom he had ordered beheaded] had been raised from the dead,
AMP.8: and by others that Elijah had appeared, and by others that one of the [other] prophets of old had been resurrected.
AMP.9: Herod said, "I personally had John beheaded. Who is this man about whom I hear such things?" And he kept trying to see Jesus.
AMP.10: When the apostles returned, they told Him all that they had done. He took them with Him and He privately withdrew [across the Jordan] to a city called Bethsaida.
AMP.11: But when the crowds learned of it, they followed Him; and He welcomed them and He began talking to them about the kingdom of God, and healing those who needed to be healed.
AMP.12: Now the day was ending, and the twelve [disciples] came and said to Him, "Send the crowd away, so that they may go into the surrounding villages and countryside and find lodging, and get provisions; because here we are in an isolated place."
AMP.13: But He said to them, "You give them something to eat." They said, "We have no more than five loaves and two fish—unless perhaps we go and buy food for all these people."
AMP.14: (For there were about 5,000 men.) And He said to His disciples, "Have them sit down to eat in groups of about fifty each."
AMP.15: They did so, and had them all sit down.
AMP.16: Then He took the five loaves and the two fish, and He looked up to heaven [and gave thanks] and blessed them, and broke them and kept giving them to the disciples to set before the crowd.
AMP.17: They all ate and were [completely] satisfied; and the broken pieces which they had left over were [abundant and were] picked up—twelve baskets full.
AMP.18: Now it happened that as Jesus was praying privately, the disciples were with Him, and He asked them, "Who do the crowds say that I am?"
AMP.19: They answered, "John the Baptist, and some say, Elijah; but others, that one of the ancient prophets has come back to life."
AMP.20: And He said to them, "But who do you say that I am?" Peter replied, "The Christ (the Messiah, the Anointed) of God!"
AMP.21: But He strictly warned and admonished them not to tell this to anyone,
AMP.22: saying, "The Son of Man must suffer many things and be rejected [as the Messiah] by the elders and chief priests and scribes (Sanhedrin, Jewish High Court), and be put to death, and on the third day be raised up [from death to life]."
AMP.23: And He was saying to them all, "If anyone wishes to follow Me [as My disciple], he must deny himself [set aside selfish interests], and take up his cross daily [expressing a willingness to endure whatever may come] and follow Me [believing in Me, conforming to My example in living and, if need be, suffering or perhaps dying because of faith in Me].
AMP.24: For whoever wishes to save his life [in this world] will [eventually] lose it [through death], but whoever loses his life [in this world] for My sake, he is the one who will save it [from the consequences of sin and separation from God].
AMP.25: For what does it profit a man if he gains the whole world [wealth, fame, success], and loses or forfeits himself?
AMP.26: For whoever is ashamed [here and now] of Me and My words, the Son of Man will be ashamed of him when He comes in His glory and the glory of the [heavenly] Father and of the holy angels.
AMP.27: But I tell you truthfully, there are some among those standing here who will not taste death before they see the kingdom of God."
AMP.28: Now about eight days after these teachings, He took along Peter and John and James and went up on the mountain to pray.
AMP.29: As He was praying, the appearance of His face became different [actually transformed], and His clothing became white and flashing with the brilliance of lightning.
AMP.30: And behold, two men were talking with Him; and they were Moses and Elijah,
AMP.31: who appeared in glory, and were speaking of His departure [from earthly life], which He was about to bring to fulfillment at Jerusalem.
AMP.32: Now Peter and those who were with him had been overcome with sleep; but when they were fully awake, they saw His glory and splendor and majesty, and the two men who were standing with Him.
AMP.33: And as these [men, Moses and Elijah] were leaving Him, Peter said to Jesus, "Master, it is delightful and good for us to be here; we should make three [sacred] tents; one for You, one for Moses, and one for Elijah"—not realizing what he was saying.
AMP.34: But even as he was saying this, a cloud formed and began to overshadow them; and they were [greatly] afraid as they entered the cloud.
AMP.35: Then a voice came out of the cloud, saying, "This is My beloved Son, My Chosen One; listen and obey and yield to Him!"
AMP.36: When the voice had ceased, Jesus was found there alone. And they kept silent, and told no one at that time any of the things which they had seen [concerning the divine manifestation].
AMP.37: On the next day, when they came down from the mountain, a large crowd met Him.
AMP.38: And a man from the crowd shouted for help, "Teacher, I beg You to look at my son, because he is my only child;
AMP.39: and a spirit seizes him, and suddenly he cries out, and it throws him into a convulsion so that he foams at the mouth; and only with [great] difficulty does it leave him, mauling and bruising him as it leaves.
AMP.40: I begged Your disciples to cast it out, but they could not."
AMP.41: Jesus answered, "You unbelieving and perverted generation! How long shall I be with you and put up with you? Bring your son here [to Me]."
AMP.42: Even while the boy was coming, the demon slammed him down and threw him into a [violent] convulsion. But Jesus rebuked the unclean spirit, and healed the boy and gave him back to his father.
AMP.43:
They were all amazed [practically overwhelmed] at the [evidence of the] greatness of God and His majesty and His wondrous work.
But while they were still awed by everything Jesus was doing, He said to His disciples,
AMP.44: "Let these words sink into your ears: the Son of Man is going to be betrayed and handed over to men [who are His enemies]."
AMP.45: However, they did not understand this statement. Its meaning was kept hidden from them so that they would not grasp it; and they were afraid to ask Him about it.
AMP.46: An argument started among them as to which of them might be the greatest [surpassing the others in esteem and authority].
AMP.47: But Jesus, knowing what they were thinking in their heart, took a child and had him stand beside Him,
AMP.48: and He told them, "Whoever welcomes this child in My name welcomes Me; and whoever welcomes Me [also] welcomes Him who sent Me; for the one who is least among all of you [that is, the one who is genuinely humble—the one with a realistic self-view]—he is the one who is [truly] great."
AMP.49: John replied, "Master, we saw someone driving out demons in Your name; and we tried to stop him because he does not follow along with us."
AMP.50: But Jesus told him, "Do not stop him; for he who is not against you is for you."
AMP.51: Now when the time was approaching for Him to be taken up [to heaven], He was determined to go to Jerusalem [to fulfill His purpose].
AMP.52: He sent messengers on ahead of Him, and they went into a Samaritan village to make arrangements for Him;
AMP.53: but the people would not welcome Him, because He was traveling toward Jerusalem.
AMP.54: When His disciples James and John saw this, they said, "Lord, do You want us to command fire to come down from heaven and destroy them?"
AMP.55: But He turned and rebuked them[and He said, "You do not know what kind of spirit you are;
AMP.56: for the Son of Man did not come to destroy men's lives, but to save them."] And they journeyed on to another village.
AMP.57: As they were going along the road, someone said to Him, "I will follow You wherever You go."
AMP.58: And Jesus told him, "Foxes have holes and the birds of the air have nests, but the Son of Man has no place to lay His head."
AMP.59: He said to another, "Follow Me [accepting Me as Master and Teacher]." But he said, "Lord, allow me first to go and bury my father."
AMP.60: But He said to him, "Allow the [spiritually] dead to bury their own dead; but as for you, go and spread the news about the kingdom of God."
AMP.61: Another also said, "I will follow You, Lord [as Your disciple]; but first let me say goodbye to those at my home."

NIV.1:

NIV.4: Whatever house you enter, stay there until you leave that town.
NIV.5: If people do not welcome you, shake the dust off your feet when you leave their town, as a testimony against them.”
NIV.6: So they set out and went from village to village, preaching the gospel and healing people everywhere.
NIV.7: Now Herod the tetrarch heard about all that was going on. And he was perplexed, because some were saying that John had been raised from the dead,
NIV.8: others that Elijah had appeared, and still others that one of the prophets of long ago had come back to life.
NIV.9: But Herod said, “I beheaded John. Who, then, is this I hear such things about?” And he tried to see him.

Jesus Feeds the Five Thousand

NIV.11: but the crowds learned about it and followed him. He welcomed them and spoke to them about the kingdom of God, and healed those who needed healing.

NIV.19: They replied, “Some say John the Baptist; others say Elijah; and still others, that one of the prophets of long ago has come back to life.”
NIV.20:

The Transfiguration

NIV.31: appeared in glorious splendor, talking with Jesus. They spoke about his departure, which he was about to bring to fulfillment at Jerusalem.

Samaritan Opposition

NIV.57:
The Cost of Following Jesus
As they were walking along the road, a man said to him, “I will follow you wherever you go.”
NIV.58: Jesus replied, “Foxes have holes and birds of the air have nests, but the Son of Man has no place to lay his head.”

NIV.60: Jesus said to him, “Let the dead bury their own dead, but you go and proclaim the kingdom of God.”

NIV.62: Jesus replied, “No one who puts his hand to the plow and looks back is fit for service in the kingdom of God.”

NRSVCE.38: Just then a man from the crowd shouted, “Teacher, I beg you to look at my son; he is my only child.
NRSVCE.39: Suddenly a spirit seizes him, and all at once he shrieks. It convulses him until he foams at the mouth; it mauls him and will scarcely leave him.
NRSVCE.40: I begged your disciples to cast it out, but they could not.”
NRSVCE.41: Jesus answered, “You faithless and perverse generation, how much longer must I be with you and bear with you? Bring your son here.”
NRSVCE.42: While he was coming, the demon dashed him to the ground in convulsions. But Jesus rebuked the unclean spirit, healed the boy, and gave him back to his father.
NRSVCE.43: And all were astounded at the greatness of God. Jesus Again Foretells His Death While everyone was amazed at all that he was doing, he said to his disciples,
NRSVCE.44: “Let these words sink into your ears: The Son of Man is going to be betrayed into human hands.”
NRSVCE.45: But they did not understand this saying; its meaning was concealed from them, so that they could not perceive it. And they were afraid to ask him about this saying.
NRSVCE.46: An argument arose among them as to which one of them was the greatest.
NRSVCE.47: But Jesus, aware of their inner thoughts, took a little child and put it by his side,
NRSVCE.48: and said to them, “Whoever welcomes this child in my name welcomes me, and whoever welcomes me welcomes the one who sent me; for the least among all of you is the greatest.”
NRSVCE.49: John answered, “Master, we saw someone casting out demons in your name, and we tried to stop him, because he does not follow with us.”
NRSVCE.50: But Jesus said to him, “Do not stop him; for whoever is not against you is for you.”
NRSVCE.51: When the days drew near for him to be taken up, he set his face to go to Jerusalem.
NRSVCE.52: And he sent messengers ahead of him. On their way they entered a village of the Samaritans to make ready for him;
NRSVCE.53: but they did not receive him, because his face was set toward Jerusalem.
NRSVCE.54: When his disciples James and John saw it, they said, “Lord, do you want us to command fire to come down from heaven and consume them?”
NRSVCE.55: But he turned and rebuked them.
NRSVCE.56: Then they went on to another village.
NRSVCE.57: As they were going along the road, someone said to him, “I will follow you wherever you go.”
NRSVCE.58: And Jesus said to him, “Foxes have holes, and birds of the air have nests; but the Son of Man has nowhere to lay his head.”
NRSVCE.59: To another he said, “Follow me.” But he said, “Lord, first let me go and bury my father.”
NRSVCE.60: But Jesus said to him, “Let the dead bury their own dead; but as for you, go and proclaim the kingdom of God.”
NRSVCE.61: Another said, “I will follow you, Lord; but let me first say farewell to those at my home.”
NRSVCE.62: Jesus said to him, “No one who puts a hand to the plow and looks back is fit for the kingdom of God.”

NET.1: After Jesus called the twelve together, he gave them power and authority over all demons and to cure diseases,
NET.2: and he sent them out to proclaim the kingdom of God and to heal the sick.
NET.3: He said to them, “Take nothing for your journey — no staff, no bag, no bread, no money, and do not take an extra tunic.
NET.4: Whatever house you enter, stay there until you leave the area.
NET.5: Wherever they do not receive you, as you leave that town, shake the dust off your feet as a testimony against them.”
NET.6: Then they departed and went throughout the villages, proclaiming the good news and healing people everywhere.
NET.7: Now Herod the tetrarch heard about everything that was happening, and he was thoroughly perplexed, because some people were saying that John had been raised from the dead,
NET.8: while others were saying that Elijah had appeared, and still others that one of the prophets of long ago had risen.
NET.9: Herod said, “I had John beheaded, but who is this about whom I hear such things?” So Herod wanted to learn about Jesus.
NET.10: When the apostles returned, they told Jesus everything they had done. Then he took them with him and they withdrew privately to a town called Bethsaida.
NET.11: But when the crowds found out, they followed him. He welcomed them, spoke to them about the kingdom of God, and cured those who needed healing.
NET.12: Now the day began to draw to a close, so the twelve came and said to Jesus, “Send the crowd away, so they can go into the surrounding villages and countryside and find lodging and food, because we are in an isolated place.”
NET.13: But he said to them, “You give them something to eat.” They replied, “We have no more than five loaves and two fish — unless we go and buy food for all these people.”
NET.14: (Now about five thousand men were there.) Then he said to his disciples, “Have them sit down in groups of about fifty each.”
NET.15: So they did as Jesus directed, and the people all sat down.
NET.16: Then he took the five loaves and the two fish, and looking up to heaven he gave thanks and broke them. He gave them to the disciples to set before the crowd.
NET.17: They all ate and were satisfied, and what was left over was picked up — twelve baskets of broken pieces.
NET.18: Once when Jesus was praying by himself, and his disciples were nearby, he asked them, “Who do the crowds say that I am?”
NET.19: They answered, “John the Baptist; others say Elijah; and still others that one of the prophets of long ago has risen.”
NET.20: Then he said to them, “But who do you say that I am?” Peter answered, “The Christ of God.”
NET.21: But he forcefully commanded them not to tell this to anyone,
NET.22: saying, “The Son of Man must suffer many things and be rejected by the elders, chief priests, and experts in the law, and be killed, and on the third day be raised.”
NET.23: Then he said to them all, “If anyone wants to become my follower, he must deny himself, take up his cross daily, and follow me.
NET.24: For whoever wants to save his life will lose it, but whoever loses his life for my sake will save it.
NET.25: For what does it benefit a person if he gains the whole world but loses or forfeits himself?
NET.26: For whoever is ashamed of me and my words, the Son of Man will be ashamed of that person when he comes in his glory and in the glory of the Father and of the holy angels.
NET.27: But I tell you most certainly, there are some standing here who will not experience death before they see the kingdom of God.”
NET.28: Now about eight days after these sayings, Jesus took with him Peter, John, and James, and went up the mountain to pray.
NET.29: As he was praying, the appearance of his face was transformed, and his clothes became very bright, a brilliant white.
NET.30: Then two men, Moses and Elijah, began talking with him.
NET.31: They appeared in glorious splendor and spoke about his departure that he was about to carry out at Jerusalem.
NET.32: Now Peter and those with him were quite sleepy, but as they became fully awake, they saw his glory and the two men standing with him.
NET.33: Then as the men were starting to leave, Peter said to Jesus, “Master, it is good for us to be here. Let us make three shelters, one for you and one for Moses and one for Elijah” — not knowing what he was saying.
NET.34: As he was saying this, a cloud came and overshadowed them, and they were afraid as they entered the cloud.
NET.35: Then a voice came from the cloud, saying, “This is my Son, my Chosen One. Listen to him!”
NET.36: After the voice had spoken, Jesus was found alone. So they kept silent and told no one at that time anything of what they had seen.
NET.37: Now on the next day, when they had come down from the mountain, a large crowd met him.
NET.38: Then a man from the crowd cried out, “Teacher, I beg you to look at my son — he is my only child!
NET.39: A spirit seizes him, and he suddenly screams; it throws him into convulsions and causes him to foam at the mouth. It hardly ever leaves him alone, torturing him severely.
NET.40: I begged your disciples to cast it out, but they could not do so.”
NET.41: Jesus answered, “You unbelieving and perverse generation! How much longer must I be with you and endure you? Bring your son here.”
NET.42: As the boy was approaching, the demon threw him to the ground and shook him with convulsions. But Jesus rebuked the unclean spirit, healed the boy, and gave him back to his father.
NET.43: Then they were all astonished at the mighty power of God. But while the entire crowd was amazed at everything Jesus was doing, he said to his disciples,
NET.44: “Take these words to heart, for the Son of Man is going to be betrayed into the hands of men.”
NET.45: But they did not understand this statement; its meaning had been concealed from them, so that they could not grasp it. Yet they were afraid to ask him about this statement.
NET.46: Now an argument started among the disciples as to which of them might be the greatest.
NET.47: But when Jesus discerned their innermost thoughts, he took a child, had him stand by his side,
NET.48: and said to them, “Whoever welcomes this child in my name welcomes me, and whoever welcomes me welcomes the one who sent me, for the one who is least among you all is the one who is great.”
NET.49: John answered, “Master, we saw someone casting out demons in your name, and we tried to stop him because he is not a disciple along with us.”
NET.50: But Jesus said to him, “Do not stop him, for whoever is not against you is for you.”
NET.51: Now when the days drew near for him to be taken up, Jesus set out resolutely to go to Jerusalem.
NET.52: He sent messengers on ahead of him. As they went along, they entered a Samaritan village to make things ready in advance for him,
NET.53: but the villagers refused to welcome him, because he was determined to go to Jerusalem.
NET.54: Now when his disciples James and John saw this, they said, “Lord, do you want us to call fire to come down from heaven and consume them?”
NET.55: But Jesus turned and rebuked them,
NET.56: and they went on to another village.
NET.57: As they were walking along the road, someone said to him, “I will follow you wherever you go.”
NET.58: Jesus said to him, “Foxes have dens and the birds in the sky have nests, but the Son of Man has no place to lay his head.”
NET.59: Jesus said to another, “Follow me.” But he replied, “Lord, first let me go and bury my father.”
NET.60: But Jesus said to him, “Let the dead bury their own dead, but as for you, go and proclaim the kingdom of God.”
NET.61: Yet another said, “I will follow you, Lord, but first let me say goodbye to my family.”
NET.62: Jesus said to him, “No one who puts his hand to the plow and looks back is fit for the kingdom of God.”
